title: Nexus: error related to optimization around comparison / ccu2.   Includes "equiv" check script.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Unpack the attached zip file [ccu2.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/8009883/ccu2.zip) and `cd` into the directory `bug`, then run:
```
yosys pool.ys
yosys eq.ys
```
The first reads `cfu.v` and produces `mapped.v` for Nexus.   The second is a fairly standard equiv checking.   It should prove equivalence.

Then, edit `pool.ys` to *remove* the `-noccu2` option from the `synth_nexus` command, and rerun the same two yosys commands.

It will not be able to prove equivalence.

The key computation is:
```
  assign \$2  = in1[7:0] ^  8'h80;
  assign \$4  = in0[7:0] ^  8'h80;
  assign \$6  = \$2  >  \$4 ;
  assign result0  = \$6  ?  in1[7:0] : in0[7:0];
```
The MSB of in0 and in1 are each inverted before preforming an unsigned comparison, to get the effect of a signed comparison.

In the original design, without `-noccu2`, we see the wrong input getting selected, as if the MSB inversion did not happen.

## Expected behavior

I would expect it to prove equivalence with or without the `-noccu2` option.

## Actual behavior

It cannot prove equivalence when `-noccu2` is *not* used.
